question: [File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with account.id=eq:<valid_account> that matches millions of schedules, causing the schedulesMainQuery to return the full limit and then trigger two additional expensive IN-clause queries, freezing transaction processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of query timeout enforcement in pool.queryQuietly to submit requests that trigger long-running DB queries, holding DB connections open for 500%+ of average block time and starving other transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: mergeScheduleEntities()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger mergeScheduleEntities() with 100 schedules each having large signature blobs, causing the Object.assign() merge loop to allocate excessive memory in the Node.js heap and trigger GC pauses that freeze transaction processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the signatureQuery's group by entity_id combined with json_agg ordering by ts.consensus_timestamp to force the DB to perform an expensive sort on a large transaction_signature table, delaying block processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getScheduleById()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly request the same schedule ID with a large number of signatures, causing repeated execution of the group by s.schedule_id, e.id aggregation query and holding DB CPU at saturation, freezing transaction processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user craft alternating asc/desc order requests to prevent query plan caching in PostgreSQL, causing repeated query plan generation overhead that cumulatively delays transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractSqlFromScheduleFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum allowed number of repeated account.id filter values to cause extractSqlFromScheduleFilters() to build a WHERE clause with many AND conditions, forcing the DB to evaluate multiple predicates per row and delaying transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the three-query fan-out in getSchedules() (schedulesQuery, entityQuery, signatureQuery) to amplify a single HTTP request into three concurrent DB queries, achieving a 3x DB load amplification that delays transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getScheduleCacheControlHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hasExpired branch (expirationTime + SIXTY_SECONDS * NANOS_PER_SECOND) in getScheduleCacheControlHeader() to cause responses for recently-expired schedules to be cached for 3600 seconds, freezing the visible state of pending transactions for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with schedule.id=gte:0 and limit=100 in a tight loop from multiple source IPs, causing the DB connection pool to be exhausted and all transaction-related API calls to queue indefinitely, freezing transaction processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: formatScheduleRow()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger formatScheduleRow() on a schedule with a null executed_timestamp and null expiration_time, causing utils.nsToSecNs() to process null values in a way that produces unexpected output and causes downstream consumers to misinterpret transaction timing, effectively freezing their view of transaction state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a LIMIT cap enforcement in extractSqlFromScheduleFilters() to request limit=100 (max) and trigger the maximum-size IN clause in entityQuery and signatureQuery, causing the DB to perform the most expensive possible query path and delaying transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getScheduleByIdQuery] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the left join entity e on e.id = s.schedule_id combined with left join transaction_signature ts on ts.entity_id = s.schedule_id to cause a Cartesian-product-like intermediate result when a schedule has many signatures, causing the DB to process millions of intermediate rows and freezing transaction processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the signatureQuery's json_agg with order by ts.consensus_timestamp to force an in-memory sort of all signatures per schedule, causing excessive memory allocation in PostgreSQL that delays transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with account.id=gte:0 without a schedule.id bound, causing the schedulesMainQuery to perform a full table scan with no upper bound, holding a long-running read transaction that blocks VACUUM and autovacuum on the schedule table and freezing transaction processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Promise.all([entityQuery, signatureQuery]) pattern to cause two simultaneous long-running queries that each hold shared locks on the entity and transaction_signature tables, blocking writes and freezing transaction ingestion for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getScheduleById()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate valid schedule IDs and flood getScheduleById() with requests for schedules that have large transaction_body blobs, causing utils.encodeBase64() to perform repeated large buffer encoding operations that saturate Node.js CPU and delay transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of connection-level query timeout in pool.queryQuietly to cause the signatureQuery's json_agg aggregation to run indefinitely on a large transaction_signature table, holding a DB connection open and freezing transaction processing for 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: extractSqlFromScheduleFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both account.id and schedule.id filters simultaneously to cause extractSqlFromScheduleFilters() to build a two-condition WHERE clause that bypasses any single-column index, forcing a full table scan and delaying transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination link generation in getPaginationLink() to craft a next-page URL that causes getSchedules() to re-execute the full three-query fan-out for each page, sustaining DB load across many sequential requests and delaying transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with schedule.id=gte:0 and account.id=gte:0 simultaneously to force the DB to evaluate both creator_account_id and s.schedule_id predicates without an optimal composite index, causing slow query execution that delays transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getScheduleByIdQuery] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the group by s.schedule_id, e.id in getScheduleByIdQuery to cause PostgreSQL to perform an expensive hash aggregate operation when the schedule has many associated signatures, holding DB CPU and delaying transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scheduleOrderQuery() function's direct interpolation of the order parameter into the SQL string to cause unexpected query plan selection, resulting in a full table scan that delays transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getSchedules()] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of requests with different account.id values to prevent PostgreSQL from reusing cached query plans, causing repeated query planning overhead that cumulatively delays transaction processing by 500%+ of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest/schedules.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: mergeScheduleEntities()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger mergeScheduleEntities() with 100 schedules where each schedule has a large signatureMap entry, causing the reduce() and Object.assign() operations to allocate a large number of JavaScript objects and trigger GC pressure that freezes the Node.js event loop for 500%+ of average block time?
